opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I've been throwing up since yesterday and couldn't keep my steroid tablets down, and now I feel like I'm about to pass out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Severe weakness, dizziness and near-fainting after missing steroid doses during a vomiting illness.

symptom_narrative.onset: vomiting since yesterday, feeling faint and extremely weak over the last few hours
symptom_narrative.duration: about 24 hours of vomiting, worsening weakness over the last 3-4 hours
symptom_narrative.character: profound weakness, dizziness on standing, nausea, abdominal pain
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I've felt since I was diagnosed with Addison's disease
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: generalised weakness with crampy abdominal pain
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying down helps the dizziness slightly
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up, and being unable to keep tablets down
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: confusion, spouse says she seems 'out of it' and slower to respond than usual
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: known Addison's disease for 6 years, on daily hydrocortisone; has had one previous hospital admission for a similar crisis years ago during a bad flu
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: too weak to stand unaided, spouse having to help her to the bathroom

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: regular daily dose, but vomited them back up multiple times over the last day and hasn't been able to keep any down

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: spouse present and very concerned, has an emergency injection kit at home that was provided by her endocrinologist for exactly this situation

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: spouse is frightened and wants to know whether to use the emergency injection kit right now

access_barriers.transport: spouse available to drive but patient may not be safe to transport without prior stabilisation
